title: FIA reveals tweaks to F1 2026 driver guidelines after Qatar meeting
summary:
The FIA has tweaked the Formula 1 driver guidelines for the 2026 campaign with a focus on more flexibility and common sense being applied in what is seen as a big win for those racing.

title: Red Bull on back foot at F1 Australian GP – as Max Verstappen expected
summary:
Max Verstappen says Red Bull’s lowly position in Formula 1’s Australian Grand Prix pecking order, following Friday practice, is no surprise to him.
Verstappen was sixth-fastest in Free Practice 2, 0.637s slower than pacesetter Oscar Piastri and 0.423s behind Mercedes’ Kimi Antonelli, while his deficit to Ferrari’s Lewis Hamilton was 0.316s.

summary:
George Russell has been given a warning and a reprimand following a pair of incidents in Free Practice 2 for Formula 1’s Australian Grand Prix.
The reprimand is the more serious sanction as five of them automatically translate into a 10-place grid penalty (provided at least four are given for a driving infringement).

title: How rear LED lights can indicate energy levels on F1 2026 cars
summary:
The 2026 Formula 1 campaign is debuting an entirely new set of regulations with changes to both the chassis and engine, the latter of which has become the biggest talking point.
That's because an F1 power unit is now more reliant on electrical energy with a near 50-50 split against the internal combustion engine, meaning battery management is playing a greater role than ever.

summary:

title: FIA cuts recoverable energy in F1 qualifying to stop extreme tactics
summary:
Energy management is at the centre of the Formula 1 2026 technical regulations. The decision to triple the power of the MGU-K compared to last year, but without the MGU-H and with a battery whose capacity has remained unchanged, has presented the teams with a very complex challenge. Even on a single qualifying lap, understanding where and how to deploy the energy requires detailed study.
